Name: UDirectionalLightComponent
Description: A light component that has parallel rays. Will provide a uniform lighting across any affected surface (eg. The Sun). This will affect all objects in the defined light-mass importance volume.

Variables.Name: bEnableLightShaftOcclusion
Variables.Description: Whether to occlude fog and atmosphere inscattering with screenspace blurred occlusion from this light.

Variables.Name: OcclusionMaskDarkness
Variables.Description: Controls how dark the occlusion masking is, a value of 1 results in no darkening term.

Variables.Name: OcclusionDepthRange
Variables.Description: Everything closer to the camera than this distance will occlude light shafts.

Variables.Name: LightShaftOverrideDirection
Variables.Description:
Can be used to make light shafts come from somewhere other than the light's actual direction.
	  This will only be used when non-zero.  It does not have to be normalized.

Variables.Name: DynamicShadowDistanceMovableLight
Variables.Description:
How far Cascaded Shadow Map dynamic shadows will cover for a movable light, measured from the camera.
	  A value of 0 disables the dynamic shadow.

Variables.Name: DynamicShadowDistanceStationaryLight
Variables.Description:
How far Cascaded Shadow Map dynamic shadows will cover for a stationary light, measured from the camera.
	  A value of 0 disables the dynamic shadow.

Variables.Name: DynamicShadowCascades
Variables.Description:
Number of cascades to split the view frustum into for the whole scene dynamic shadow.
	  More cascades result in better shadow resolution, but adds significant rendering cost.

Variables.Name: CascadeDistributionExponent
Variables.Description:
Controls whether the cascades are distributed closer to the camera (larger exponent) or further from the camera (smaller exponent).
	  An exponent of 1 means that cascade transitions will happen at a distance proportional to their resolution.

Variables.Name: CascadeTransitionFraction
Variables.Description:
Proportion of the fade region between cascades.
	  Pixels within the fade region of two cascades have their shadows blended to avoid hard transitions between quality levels.
	  A value of zero eliminates the fade region, creating hard transitions.
	  Higher values increase the size of the fade region, creating a more gradual transition between cascades.
	  The value is expressed as a percentage proportion (i.e. 0.1 = 10% overlap).
	  Ideal values are the smallest possible which still hide the transition.
	  An increased fade region size causes an increase in shadow rendering cost.

Variables.Name: ShadowDistanceFadeoutFraction
Variables.Description:
Controls the size of the fade out region at the far extent of the dynamic shadow's influence.
	  This is specified as a fraction of DynamicShadowDistance.

Variables.Name: ShadowCenterOffset
Variables.Description: Offset of the CSM shadow center in the viewing direction.

Variables.Name: bUseInsetShadowsForMovableObjects
Variables.Description:
Stationary lights only: Whether to use per-object inset shadows for movable components, even though cascaded shadow maps are enabled.
	  This allows dynamic objects to have a shadow even when they are outside of the cascaded shadow map, which is important when DynamicShadowDistanceStationaryLight is small.
	  If DynamicShadowDistanceStationaryLight is large (currently > 8000), this will be forced off.
	  Disabling this can reduce shadowing cost significantly with many movable objects.

Variables.Name: FarShadowCascadeCount
Variables.Description: 0: no DistantShadowCascades, otherwise the count of cascades between WholeSceneDynamicShadowRadius and DistantShadowDistance that are covered by distant shadow cascades.

Variables.Name: FarShadowDistance
Variables.Description: Distance at which the far shadow cascade should end.  Far shadows will cover the range between 'Dynamic Shadow Distance' and this distance.

Variables.Name: DistanceFieldShadowDistance
Variables.Description: Distance at which the ray traced shadow cascade should end.  Distance field shadows will cover the range between 'Dynamic Shadow Distance' this distance.

Variables.Name: ForwardShadingPriority
Variables.Description:
Forward lighting priority for the single directional light that will be used for forward shading, translucent, single layer water and volumetric fog.
	 When two lights have equal priorities, the selection will be based on their overall brightness as a fallback.

Variables.Name: LightSourceAngle
Variables.Description:
Light source angle in degrees, used for dynamic shadowing methods.
	  Currently only Ray Traced Distance Field shadows and Capsule shadows support area shadows, and therefore make use of LightSourceAngle.

Variables.Name: TraceDistance
Variables.Description: Determines how far shadows can be cast, in world units.  Larger values increase the shadowing cost.

Variables.Name: LightmassSettings
Variables.Description: The Lightmass settings for this object.

Variables.Name: bCastModulatedShadows
Variables.Description: Whether the light should cast modulated shadows from dynamic objects (mobile only).  Also requires Cast Shadows to be set to True.

Variables.Name: bCastPhotonShadow
Variables.Description:
#if WITH_PHOTON_SHADOW
	 Whether the light should cast photon shadow for character
	 #endif

Variables.Name: ModulatedShadowColor
Variables.Description: Color to modulate against the scene color when rendering modulated shadows. (mobile only)
